title: Fixing React Function Component Update Bug

statement.markdown: You are given a React function component that displays a counter. However, the counter is not updating correctly. Your task is to fix the bug in the given code. The component should update the counter when the button is clicked.
statement.outputFormat: The final counter value should be printed to the console.
statement.notes: Make sure to handle the scenario where the button is clicked multiple times.

examples.explanation: The counter is incremented when the button is clicked.

hints.text: Check if the state is being updated correctly.

editorial.title: Fixing React Function Component Update Bug
editorial.intuition: The state update is not being handled correctly. Make sure to use the correct state update method.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use the console to debug the state value.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the setState method to update the state.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The counter should update correctly when the button is clicked.
editorial.dryRun: Step 1: Initialize the state with a counter value. Step 2: Use the setState method to update the state when the button is clicked.
commonMistakes: Not handling the state update correctly
